puzzle.title: The Honor of a Mentioned Name
puzzle.intro: A life of devotion and proximity to the final Prophet marked this individual's journey. Once bound by earthly ties, they were freed and elevated to a status of immense love and trust. Their personal story became so pivotal that it addressed a societal norm, and their name was uniquely immortalized in the divine text, a distinction no other companion received. Who is this honored soul whose name is etched in revelation?
clues: I was once a captive, but found freedom and a new family in the household of the most beloved of creation.
The Prophet of Allah loved me so dearly that people began to call me by his family's name, as if I were his own child.
My personal life became the subject of divine revelation, clarifying a matter of adoption and marriage for the entire community to follow.
The marriage that followed my divorce was ordained by Allah to abolish a pre-Islamic custom regarding adopted children.
I was entrusted with command in the Battle of Mu'tah, a responsibility I carried until my final moments.
I am the only companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ whose name is explicitly mentioned in the Quran.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq
Salman Al-Farsi
Zayd ibn Harithah
Bilal ibn Rabah
'Abdullah ibn Umm Maktum

trial.label: The Divine Decree
options: Accusation of slander against a Prophet's spouse
Marriage and divorce addressed to abolish a taboo
Suggesting the digging of a trench for defense
Being the first martyr in a major battle
Leading the project to compile the Quran
trial.answer: Marriage and divorce addressed to abolish a taboo

location.label: The Social Custom Addressed
options: The rules of inheritance for orphans
The prohibition of interest (Riba)
The establishment of the Hijri calendar
The status of an adopted son
The rituals of the Hajj pilgrimage
location.answer: The status of an adopted son

outcome.label: The Ultimate Honor
options: Being the only companion named in the Quran
Becoming the first Caliph after the Prophet's death
Being given the title 'The Sword of Allah'
Being promised Paradise while building a mosque
Being the first Muezzin of Islam
outcome.answer: Being the only companion named in the Quran

puzzle.arabic: وَإِذْ تَقُولُ لِلَّذِىٓ أَنْعَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَأَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِ أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ وَٱتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ وَتُخْفِى فِى نَفْسِكَ مَا ٱللَّهُ مُبْدِيهِ وَتَخْشَى ٱلنَّاسَ وَٱللَّهُ أَحَقُّ أَن تَخْشَىٰهُ ۖ فَلَمَّا قَضَىٰ زَيْدٌۭ مِّنْهَا وَطَرًۭا زَوَّجْنَـٰكَهَا لِكَىْ لَا يَكُونَ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ حَرَجٌۭ فِىٓ أَزْوَٰجِ أَدْعِيَآئِهِمْ إِذَا قَضَوْا۟ مِنْهُنَّ وَطَرًۭا ۚ وَكَانَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ مَفْعُولًۭا
puzzle.verse: And [remember, O Muḥammad], when you said to the one on whom Allāh bestowed favor and you bestowed favor, "Keep your wife and fear Allāh," while you concealed within yourself that which Allāh is to disclose. And you feared the people, while Allāh has more right that you fear Him. So when Zayd had no longer any need for her, We married her to you in order that there not be upon the believers any discomfort [i.e., guilt] concerning the wives of their claimed [i.e., adopted] sons when they no longer have need of them. And ever is the command [i.e., decree] of Allāh accomplished. (33:37)
